stem: Two nearby flagpoles are perpendicular to flat ground. One flagpole is 12 feet tall and casts a shadow that is 4 feet long. At the same time, the second flagpole casts a shadow that is 3 feet long. How tall, in feet, is the second flagpole?
rationale:
Choice A is correct. Each flagpole and its shadow form similar right triangles. Letting x be the height of the second pole, 12/4 = x/3, so x = 9 feet.

Choice B results from subtracting the shadow lengths from 12. Choice C is the sum 12+4. Choice D results from multiplying 12 by 3 instead of using the proportion.
